image_style: full-bleed editorial food photography with the finished dish filling most of the vertical frame
photo_composition: vertical close-up with generous negative space in the upper third for headline text and the food hero occupying the lower two-thirds
text_style: high-contrast editorial serif with oversized stacked uppercase headline and smaller supporting line
overlay_style: minimal; black headline directly over a light or softly blurred image area, with optional pale rectangular subheadline strip
layout_recommendation: Top eyebrow line at 8-12% height; oversized two-to-three-line recipe headline from 12-31%; short supporting line around 31-36%; full food image below; narrow brand footer at bottom

headline_rules.eyebrow: Use 2-4 short words such as HIGH PROTEIN, EASY DINNER, or 40/400 MEAL
headline_rules.main_headline: Use the shortest recognizable recipe name possible, ideally 2-5 words, uppercase, stacked across 2-3 lines
headline_rules.supporting_line: Use one concise benefit or nutrition statement, no more than 8 words
headline_rules.font_character: bold editorial serif with strong mobile readability
headline_rules.alignment: centered or slightly left-aligned depending on image negative space
headline_rules.safe_margins: Keep all text at least 6% from the left and right edges

image_rules.subject_scale: The finished food should occupy approximately 55-70% of the pin
image_rules.background: Natural tabletop or kitchen setting with subtle texture and shallow depth of field
image_rules.negative_space: Preserve a calm uncluttered upper third for typography
image_rules.lighting: Soft natural side light with appetizing texture and realistic color
avoid: extra ingredients not present in the recipe

brand_footer.background: warm neutral sampled from the image or brand palette
brand_footer.text_style: small uppercase sans serif with generous letter spacing
